payload.name: So lets chat before we start this. I have been building
payload.description:
So lets chat before we start this. I have been building this app for some time now. I have learnt how to         
  vibecode better so I created two mds to be my focal point MASTER_CHECKLIST.md and VIBECODING_BLUEPRINT.md. So    
  you will refer to them first. Another thing I wanna point out i have given you everything you would need to      
  access this like supabase project you have my permission. So I have tried to make a PROGRESS.md but if you see   
  anything missing on your end you can fix that too. I want you to approach this project as a homebrewer so you    
  should check what the user expects then develop it. When a user clicks here what do they expect. I have added playwright and chromium and some credentials in the .env.local for you to full explore the app.

memory.content: Project: Homebrew Haven — fermentation batch tracker + social community. Stack: React + Vite + TypeScript + Supabase + TanStack Query + shadcn/ui + Tailwind. Currently in Phase 9 mid-build per MASTER_CHECKLIST.md. Auth works, many hooks/pages built. Known critical gaps: RLS unverified, useFollowedPosts bug in Community.tsx, no copy.js, placeholder DB types, notification bell unclickable, FK aliases unverified, RPC/trigger/schema items not verified. User provided Supabase credentials + test accounts in .env.local and wants homebrewer UX exploration.

goal.answer: Audit Homebrew Haven for critical runtime bugs and schema gaps, then fix everything found. Scope covers: frontend runtime crashes (Community Following tab, posts→profiles join, follows→profiles join, notification bell), missing database constraints and columns (follows FKs, posts fts), and RLS gaps on all Supabase tables. Finish with a full app walkthrough using the provided test credentials.

payload.plain: Audit Homebrew Haven for critical runtime bugs and schema gaps, then fix everything found. Scope covers: frontend runtime crashes (Community Following tab, posts→profiles join, follows→profiles join, notification bell), missing database constraints and columns (follows FKs, posts fts), and RLS gaps on all Supabase tables. Finish with a full app walkthrough using the provided test credentials.

criteria.answer:
Community page 'Following' tab crash is fixed — useFollowedPosts called correctly in Community.tsx and the tab renders without runtime errors.
App manually explored via browser/Playwright with test credentials; every major route visited; console errors and broken UX documented and critical runtime errors fixed.
RLS enabled and verified on every table listed in PROGRESS.md (profiles, recipes, batches, readings, batch_stages, posts, post_likes, comments, challenges, challenge_entries, tasting_sessions, tasting_messages, tasting_notes, notifications, follows, yeast_bank); missing policies created where gaps exist.
Real Supabase-generated TypeScript types replace the hand-written placeholder in src/types/database.ts (generated via npx supabase gen types typescript from the live project).
FK aliases in useFollows.ts match actual Supabase schema names after adding missing foreign-key constraints on follows.follower_id and follows.followed_id.
Required database objects verified or created: toggle_post_like RPC, handle_new_user trigger on auth.users, and fts tsvector/generated column on posts table.
npm run build completes with zero errors and npm run test passes (or test failures are explained and fixed if related to this work).

plain: Community page 'Following' tab crash is fixed — useFollowedPosts called correctly in Community.tsx and the tab renders without runtime errors.
App manually explored via browser/Playwright with test credentials; every major route visited; console errors and broken UX documented and critical runtime errors fixed.

RLS enabled and verified on every table listed in PROGRESS.md (profiles, recipes, batches, readings, batch_stages, posts, post_likes, comments, challenges, challenge_entries, tasting_sessions, tasting_messages, tasting_notes, notifications, follows, yeast_bank); missing policies created where gaps exist.
Real Supabase-generated TypeScript types replace the hand-written placeholder in src/types/database.ts (generated via npx supabase gen types typescript from the live project).
FK aliases in useFollows.ts match actual Supabase schema names after adding missing foreign-key constraints on follows.follower_id and follows.followed_id.
Required database objects verified or created: toggle_post_like RPC, handle_new_user trigger on auth.users, and fts tsvector/generated column on posts table.
npm run build completes with zero errors and npm run test passes (or test failures are explained and fixed if related to this work).

constraints.answer: Do not break existing test suite — all 37 current tests must still pass after fixes., Schema changes must use Supabase migrations and npx supabase db push; do not modify the database via raw SQL outside of tracked migrations., Preserve all existing data in the Supabase dev project., Follow the existing codebase conventions (hooks in src/hooks/, Supabase logic never in components, shadcn/ui components).

plain: Do not break existing test suite — all 37 current tests must still pass after fixes.
Schema changes must use Supabase migrations and npx supabase db push; do not modify the database via raw SQL outside of tracked migrations.
Preserve all existing data in the Supabase dev project.
Follow the existing codebase conventions (hooks in src/hooks/, Supabase logic never in components, shadcn/ui components).

phases.answer: Generate Real Types + Frontend Critical Fixes, Schema Security + Integrity, App Exploration & Verification

phaseSnapshots.name: Generate Real Types + Frontend Critical Fixes
phaseSnapshots.goal: Fix immediate frontend runtime bugs that crash or silently fail, and replace placeholder database types with real Supabase-generated types.

steps.description: Generate real Supabase TypeScript types via npx supabase gen types typescript --linked and replace src/types/database.ts
Fix Community.tsx to remove invalid { enabled } argument passed to useFollowedPosts (hook takes no parameters)
Fix usePosts.ts to use profiles!user_id(column) hint instead of bare profiles because PostgREST detects multiple relationships for posts→profiles
Fix any TypeScript compilation errors introduced by switching from placeholder types to real generated types

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' | xargs -I{} test {} -eq 0

phaseSnapshots.name: Schema Security + Integrity
phaseSnapshots.goal: Add missing database constraints (follows FKs, posts fts column), enable RLS on every table, and create ownership-based policies so anon-users can't read private data.

steps.description: Create Supabase migration adding FK constraints: follows.follower_id → profiles.id and follows.followed_id → profiles.id with ON DELETE CASCADE

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && npx supabase migration list 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'follows'

steps.description: Create Supabase migration adding posts.fts generated tsvector column + GIN index for full-text search

verification.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import ws from "ws"; import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"; const c=createClient("https://zvdkwjexmseczzpqkwyb.supabase.co","eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Inp2ZGt3amV4bXNlY3p6cHFrd3liIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4MTAyNzk0MCwiZXhwIjoyMDk2NjAzOTQwfQ.u3ucIjJ4zew0Z8_L5uADM85fXTQOaNUQLAJX0D-nZfc",{realtime:{transport:ws,enabled:false},auth:{autoRefreshToken:false,persistSession:false}}); const r=await c.from("posts").select("*").textSearch("fts","test").limit(1); if(r.error&&r.error.message.includes("does not exist")) process.exit(1); else process.exit(0);'

steps.description: Create Supabase migration enabling RLS on all 17 tables and adding SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ownership policies

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && npx supabase migration list 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'rls'

steps.description: Apply all migrations via npx supabase db push

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && mv .env.local .env.local.bak && T=$(grep SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN .env.local.bak | cut -d= -f2- | xargs) && SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN="$T" npx supabase migration list 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'applied' | xargs -I{} test {} -ge 1; mv .env.local.bak .env.local

steps.description: Update useFollows.ts FK aliases to match the newly created constraint names after migration

verification.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import ws from "ws"; import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"; const c=createClient("https://zvdkwjexmseczzpqkwyb.supabase.co","eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Inp2ZGt3amV4bXNlY3p6cHFrd3liIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4MTAyNzk0MCwiZXhwIjoyMDk2NjAzOTQwfQ.u3ucIjJ4zew0Z8_L5uADM85fXTQOaNUQLAJX0D-nZfc",{realtime:{transport:ws,enabled:false},auth:{autoRefreshToken:false,persistSession:false}}); const r=await c.from("follows").select("*, profiles!follows_follower_id_fkey(username, display_name, avatar_url)").limit(1); if(r.error) process.exit(1); else process.exit(0);'

steps.description: Verify RLS is effective by checking that anon-key requests to profiles, recipes, posts, follows return no data while service-role requests return data

verification.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import ws from "ws"; import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"; const url="https://zvdkwjexmseczzpqkwyb.supabase.co"; const a="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Inp2ZGt3amV4bXNlY3p6cHFrd3liIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3ODEwMjc5NDAsImV4cCI6MjA5NjYwMzk0MH0.NwJ7pl2qGWC93rKoCO5O_x3hLBP90euL-gSDKyFrnjI"; const s="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Inp2ZGt3amV4bXNlY3p6cHFrd3liIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4MTAyNzk0MCwiZXhwIjoyMDk2NjAzOTQwfQ.u3ucIjJ4zew0Z8_L5uADM85fXTQOaNUQLAJX0D-nZfc"; const o={realtime:{transport:ws,enabled:false},auth:{autoRefreshToken:false,persistSession:false}}; const ac=createClient(url,a,o); const sc=createClient(url,s,o); for(const t of ["profiles","recipes","posts","tasting_sessions","follows"]){ const sr=(await sc.from(t).select("*",{count:"exact",head:true})).count??0; const an=(await ac.from(t).select("*",{count:"exact",head:true})).count??0; if(sr>0&&an>0){ console.log(t+" RLS FAIL"); process.exit(1); } } console.log("RLS OK"); process.exit(0);'

phaseSnapshots.name: App Exploration & Verification
phaseSnapshots.goal: Wire up the notification bell, explore the app as a homebrewer with test credentials, fix any runtime issues discovered, and verify build + tests.

steps.description: Add notification bell click handler in AppLayout.tsx that opens a dropdown of notifications with mark-as-read action
Build the app and check for zero TypeScript/build errors

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' | xargs -I{} test {} -eq 0

steps.description: Run the full test suite and verify all tests pass
Run app walkthrough via Playwright or dev server + browser with test credentials; verify every major route loads without runtime errors

verification.command: cd /project/workspace && npm run dev & sleep 8 && curl -s http://localhost:8080 | grep -q 'Homebrew Haven'

assumptions.answer: User has explicitly given permission to modify the Supabase project schema and use the provided credentials.; The service role key has sufficient privileges for schema migrations (CREATE, ALTER, RLS).; Node.js v20 with ws package installed is sufficient for Supabase client in Node scripts.; The notification bell should open a dropdown showing recent notifications with a mark-as-read action (a safe default UX).; RLS policies will follow standard ownership-based access: users can read public data, users can CRUD their own rows, admins can override.

payload.summary: Phase 1 complete: generated real Supabase TypeScript types, fixed Community.tsx useFollowedPosts invalid call, fixed usePosts.ts posts→profiles relationship hint, and verified the build still passes cleanly with zero TypeScript errors.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 2 complete: added FK constraints on follows (follower_id, followed_id → profiles.id), added posts.fts generated tsvector column with GIN index, enabled RLS with ownership policies on all 16 public tables, verified FK aliases resolve and RLS blocks unauthorized access.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 3 complete: wired up notification bell dropdown with mark-as-read and navigation, verified build passes with zero TS errors, all 37 tests pass, and Playwright walkthrough of all major routes confirmed zero runtime errors. Discovered and fixed a Community.tsx bug where usePosts return shape was treated as an array.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.finalSummary: Ferment 'Stop the Bleeding' is complete. Audit found and fixed critical frontend runtime bugs (Community Following tab crash, posts→profiles join hint, follows→profiles missing FK constraints, notification bell unclickable) and schema gaps (RLS disabled on 5+ tables, missing posts.fts column). Applied 3 Supabase migrations (follows FKs, posts fts, RLS + policies on all 16 tables). Verified with live DB queries, Playwright walkthrough, build, and tests.
